clinical_history: ECG from an elderly man with a very rapid ventricular rate and palpitations. Which ONE is the likely diagnosis?
diagnosis: The ECG shows atrial fibrillation with a very rapid ventricular response (average ventricular rate about 125/min; range about 80-180/min). An intraventricular conduction delay is present, along with marked left axis deviation (close to -50 degrees), the latter finding consistent with left anterior fascicular block. There is a delayed R wave transition zone, with non-specific ST-T changes. Note that the intermittently large amplitude fibrillatory (f) waves (e.g., in lead V1)  may simulate flutter or even P waves. In other leads, very low amplitude, fast f waves may be overlooked leading to misdiagnosis.
